ops.title: Disk is the record: codex-ask lifecycle invariants
ops.body:
`codex-ask` reconstructs a run's state from on-disk artifacts, never from what an agent says. These invariants are load-bearing ‚Äî a change that breaks one silently corrupts fan-out reconciliation or loses/mis-holds finished work. Preserve them (and the test battery in `plugins/repo-bootstrap/tests/test_scaffold.py -k "codex_ask or disk_truth"`).

## Lifecycle invariants
- **Non-empty `$R` ‚áî codex exited 0 with a complete reply.** codex writes to `-o "$R.tmp"`; the reply is published (`mv "$R.tmp" "$R"`) only under `[ "$rc" -eq 0 ] && [ -s "$R.tmp" ]`, and strictly BEFORE the status write. Do not let a nonzero-exit or partial run populate `$R` ‚Äî recovery keys on `$R` to synthesize a durable `status=0`, so a false non-empty `$R` fakes success.
- **`meta` present ‚áí the latest launch attempt.** The reuse wipe removes `status`, `pid`, AND `meta` before writing fresh `meta`; codex launches strictly after `meta` is written. So "no meta ‚áí no codex ran this attempt ‚áí redo is safe, never double-pays."
- **`--collect` is read-only and disk-only.** It never writes, blocks, or inlines reply contents (path + size only). States mirror poll_status/report_status. A minted-but-never-run lane must read `no-run` (roster visibility), not vanish.
- **Death detection is by ABSENCE.** A clean codex `--json` run emits `{"type":"turn.started"}` ‚Ä¶ `{"type":"turn.completed",...}`. A mid-turn death emits neither terminal marker (the log just stops). `report_status` flags died-mid-turn on `turn.started` present + `turn.completed` absent. Grep LITERALS ‚Äî the JSONL stream interleaves stray non-JSON Rust tracing lines, so never full-parse. The grep is intentionally unanchored and fails OPEN (an event rename ‚Üí reports success, never a false died that would break every call).
- **Print-first + detach = timeout survival.** `set -m` + the `( { ‚Ä¶ } & )` braced group orphan codex to PID 1 so a Bash-tool pgid/descendant kill can't destroy finished work; `REPLY_FILE:`/`LOG_FILE:`/`AWAIT:` print before launch so a killed call still carries its `--await` recovery recipe. Do not disturb this block; edits go INSIDE the braced group only.

## Known, sanctioned deferrals (do not "fix" without weighing)
- **pid-recycle**: liveness is `kill -0` on the recorded pid, not disk-derived. An external kill (OOM/reboot ‚Äî not the Bash timeout, which the orphan escapes) plus PID reuse can mislabel a dead lane `running` / hang `--await`. Rare, same-user short-lived roots; the fix if it bites is a `started`-timestamp file.
- **concurrent shared `-s`**: sanctioned last-writer-wins (`question_files > 1` is the tell). The protocol avoids it by minting a UNIQUE lane dir per agent.
- **codex `-o` ENOSPC-with-exit-0**: codex can leave a truncated `-o` file yet exit 0 with turn.completed ‚Äî undetectable from bash (upstream codex).

See note df0c3f4 for the shipping decision, C1/C3 experiment verdicts, and the full finder/refuter review disposition.
ops.when: before changing codex-ask lifecycle, --collect classification, staged reply, poll recovery, or structured-output handling
